fields.name: device_id
fields.description: Fixed device ID code of 0xE5 (11100101 binary, 345 octal).

fields.name: thresh_tap
fields.description: Threshold value for tap interrupts. Unsigned data format. Scale factor: 62.5 mg/LSB (0xFF = +16 g). A value of 0 may result in undesirable behavior if tap/double tap interrupts are enabled.

fields.name: ofsx
fields.description: X-axis offset adjustment in twos complement format with a scale factor of 15.6 mg/LSB (0x7F = +2 g).

fields.name: ofsy
fields.description: Y-axis offset adjustment in twos complement format with a scale factor of 15.6 mg/LSB (0x7F = +2 g).

fields.name: z_offset
fields.description: Z-axis offset adjustment in twos complement format with a scale factor of 15.6 mg/LSB (0x7F = +2 g).

fields.name: dur
fields.description: Unsigned time value representing the maximum time that an event must be above the THRESH_TAP threshold to qualify as a tap event. The scale factor is 625 μs/LSB. A value of 0 disables the tap/double tap functions.

fields.name: latent
fields.description: Unsigned time value representing the wait time from the detection of a tap event to the start of the time window (defined by the window register) during which a possible second tap event can be detected. Scale factor is 1.25 ms/LSB. A value of 0 disables the double tap function.

fields.name: window
fields.description: Unsigned time value representing the amount of time after the expiration of the latency time (determined by the latent register) during which a second valid tap can begin. The scale factor is 1.25 ms/LSB. A value of 0 disables the double tap function.

fields.name: thresh_act
fields.description: Threshold value for detecting activity. The data format is unsigned, so the magnitude of the activity event is compared with the value in the THRESH_ACT register. The scale factor is 62.5 mg/LSB. A value of 0 may result in undesirable behavior if the activity interrupt is enabled.

fields.name: thresh_inact
fields.description: Threshold value for detecting inactivity. The data format is unsigned, so the magnitude of the inactivity event is compared with the value in the THRESH_INACT register. The scale factor is 62.5 mg/LSB. A value of 0 mg may result in undesirable behavior if the inactivity interrupt is enabled.

fields.name: time_inact
fields.description: Unsigned time value representing the amount of time that acceleration must be less than the value in the THRESH_INACT register for inactivity to be declared. The scale factor is 1 sec/LSB. Uses filtered output data. A value of 0 results in an interrupt when the output data is less than the value in the THRESH_INACT register.

fields.name: act_acdc
fields.description: ACT AC/DC coupling selection. 0 selects DC-coupled operation. 1 enables AC-coupled operation.

fields.name: act_x_en
fields.description: ACT_X enable. 1 enables X-axis participation in activity detection. 0 excludes X-axis.

fields.name: act_y_en
fields.description: ACT_Y enable. 1 enables Y-axis participation in activity detection. 0 excludes Y-axis.

fields.name: act_z_en
fields.description: ACT_Z enable. 1 enables Z-axis participation in activity detection. 0 excludes Z-axis.

fields.name: inact_acdc
fields.description: INACT AC/DC coupling selection. 0 selects DC-coupled operation. 1 enables AC-coupled operation.

fields.name: inact_x_en
fields.description: INACT_X enable. 1 enables X-axis participation in inactivity detection. 0 excludes X-axis.

fields.name: inact_y_en
fields.description: INACT_Y enable. 1 enables Y-axis participation in inactivity detection. 0 excludes Y-axis.

fields.name: inact_z_en
fields.description: INACT_Z enable. 1 enables Z-axis participation in inactivity detection. 0 excludes Z-axis.

fields.name: thresh_ff
fields.description: Holds the threshold value, in unsigned format, for free-fall detection. The root-sum-square (RSS) value of all axes is calculated and compared with the value in THRESH_FF to determine if a free-fall event occurred. The scale factor is 62.5 mg/LSB. A value of 0 mg may result in undesirable behavior if the free-fall interrupt is enabled. Values between 300 mg and 600 mg (0x05 to 0x09) are recommended.

fields.name: time_ff
fields.description: Unsigned time value representing the minimum time that the RSS value of all axes must be less than THRESH_FF to generate a free-fall interrupt. The scale factor is 5 ms/LSB.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved bits.

fields.name: suppress
fields.description: Suppress bit. Setting the suppress bit suppresses double tap detection if acceleration greater than the value in THRESH_TAP is present between taps.

fields.name: tap_x_en
fields.description: TAP_X enable bit. A setting of 1 enables x-axis participation in tap detection. A setting of 0 excludes the x-axis from participation in tap detection.

fields.name: tap_y_en
fields.description: TAP_Y enable bit. A setting of 1 enables y-axis participation in tap detection. A setting of 0 excludes the y-axis from participation in tap detection.

fields.name: tap_z_en
fields.description: TAP_Z enable bit. A setting of 1 enables z-axis participation in tap detection. A setting of 0 excludes the z-axis from participation in tap detection.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved bit, always reads as 0.

fields.name: act_x_source
fields.description: Activity source for X-axis. A setting of 1 indicates the X-axis was the first axis involved in an activity event; 0 indicates no involvement.

fields.name: act_y_source
fields.description: Activity source for Y-axis. A setting of 1 indicates the Y-axis was the first axis involved in an activity event; 0 indicates no involvement.

fields.name: act_z_source
fields.description: Activity source for Z-axis. A setting of 1 indicates the Z-axis was the first axis involved in an activity event; 0 indicates no involvement.

fields.name: asleep
fields.description: Asleep status bit. A setting of 1 indicates that the part is asleep; 0 indicates that the part is not asleep.

fields.name: tap_x_source
fields.description: Tap source for X-axis. A setting of 1 indicates the X-axis was the first axis involved in a tap or double tap event; 0 indicates no involvement.

fields.name: tap_y_source
fields.description: Tap source for Y-axis. A setting of 1 indicates the Y-axis was the first axis involved in a tap or double tap event; 0 indicates no involvement.

fields.name: tap_z_source
fields.description: Tap source for Z-axis. A setting of 1 indicates the Z-axis was the first axis involved in a tap or double tap event; 0 indicates no involvement.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved bits. Must be set to 0.

fields.name: low_power
fields.description: LOW_POWER bit. A setting of 0 selects normal operation. A setting of 1 selects reduced power operation, which has somewhat higher noise.

fields.name: rate
fields.description: Rate bits. These bits select the device bandwidth and output data rate. The default value is 0x0A (bits 1010), which translates to a 100 Hz output data rate.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved bits. Must be written with 0.

fields.name: link
fields.description: A setting of 1 links activity and inactivity detection serially: delays activity until inactivity detected, then inactivity after activity. 0 enables concurrent operation.

fields.name: auto_sleep
fields.description: If Link=1, 1 enables auto-switch to sleep on inactivity detection; 0 disables.

fields.name: measure
fields.description: 0=standby mode (low power); 1=measurement mode.

fields.name: sleep
fields.description: 0=normal operation; 1=sleep mode (suppresses DATA_READY, stops FIFO data, uses wakeup frequency; only activity detection active).

fields.name: wakeup
fields.description: Sets sleep mode sampling frequency: 00=8Hz, 01=4Hz, 10=2Hz, 11=1Hz (see Table 17).

fields.name: data_ready
fields.description: Enables DATA_READY interrupt. Setting to 1 enables interrupt generation when new data is available. This bit enables only the interrupt output; the function is always enabled.

fields.name: single_tap
fields.description: Enables SINGLE_TAP interrupt. Setting to 1 enables interrupt generation on single tap detection.

fields.name: double_tap
fields.description: Enables DOUBLE_TAP interrupt. Setting to 1 enables interrupt generation on double tap detection.

fields.name: activity
fields.description: Enables Activity interrupt. Setting to 1 enables interrupt generation on activity detection.

fields.name: inactivity
fields.description: Enables Inactivity interrupt. Setting to 1 enables interrupt generation on inactivity detection.

fields.name: free_fall
fields.description: Enables FREE_FALL interrupt. Setting to 1 enables interrupt generation on free-fall detection.

fields.name: watermark
fields.description: Enables Watermark interrupt. Setting to 1 enables interrupt generation when FIFO watermark is reached. This bit enables only the interrupt output; the function is always enabled.

fields.name: overrun
fields.description: Enables Overrun interrupt. Setting to 1 enables interrupt generation when FIFO overrun occurs. This bit enables only the interrupt output; the function is always enabled.

fields.name: data_ready
fields.description: Interrupt mapping for DATA_READY: 0 sends interrupt to INT1 pin, 1 sends to INT2 pin

fields.name: single_tap
fields.description: Interrupt mapping for SINGLE_TAP: 0 sends interrupt to INT1 pin, 1 sends to INT2 pin

fields.name: double_tap
fields.description: Interrupt mapping for DOUBLE_TAP: 0 sends interrupt to INT1 pin, 1 sends to INT2 pin

fields.name: activity
fields.description: Interrupt mapping for Activity: 0 sends interrupt to INT1 pin, 1 sends to INT2 pin

fields.name: inactivity
fields.description: Interrupt mapping for Inactivity: 0 sends interrupt to INT1 pin, 1 sends to INT2 pin

fields.name: free_fall
fields.description: Interrupt mapping for FREE_FALL: 0 sends interrupt to INT1 pin, 1 sends to INT2 pin

fields.name: watermark
fields.description: Interrupt mapping for Watermark: 0 sends interrupt to INT1 pin, 1 sends to INT2 pin

fields.name: overrun
fields.description: Interrupt mapping for Overrun: 0 sends interrupt to INT1 pin, 1 sends to INT2 pin

fields.name: data_ready
fields.description: Set to 1 when new data is available. Cleared by reading DATAX, DATAY, DATAZ registers.

fields.name: single_tap
fields.description: Set to 1 when a single tap event occurs. Cleared by reading INT_SOURCE register.

fields.name: double_tap
fields.description: Set to 1 when a double tap event occurs. Cleared by reading INT_SOURCE register.

fields.name: activity
fields.description: Set to 1 when an activity event occurs. Cleared by reading INT_SOURCE register.

fields.name: inactivity
fields.description: Set to 1 when an inactivity event occurs. Cleared by reading INT_SOURCE register.

fields.name: free_fall
fields.description: Set to 1 when a free-fall event occurs. Cleared by reading INT_SOURCE register.

fields.name: watermark
fields.description: Set to 1 when FIFO watermark level is reached. Cleared by reading DATAX, DATAY, DATAZ registers.

fields.name: overrun
fields.description: Set to 1 when FIFO overrun occurs. Cleared by reading DATAX, DATAY, DATAZ registers.

fields.name: self_test
fields.description: A setting of 1 in the SELF_TEST bit applies a self-test force to the sensor, causing a shift in the output data. A value of 0 disables the self-test force.

fields.name: spi
fields.description: A value of 1 in the SPI bit sets the device to 3-wire SPI mode, and a value of 0 sets the device to 4-wire SPI mode.

fields.name: int_invert
fields.description: A value of 0 in the INT_INVERT bit sets the interrupts to active high, and a value of 1 sets the interrupts to active low.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved bit that must be set to 0.

fields.name: full_res
fields.description: When this bit is set to a value of 1, the device is in full resolution mode, where the output resolution increases with the g range set by the range bits to maintain a 4 mg/LSB scale factor. When the FULL_RES bit is set to 0, the device is in 10-bit mode, and the range bits determine the maximum g range and scale factor.

fields.name: justify
fields.description: A setting of 1 in the justify bit selects left (MSB) justified mode, and a setting of 0 selects right justified mode with sign extension.

fields.name: range
fields.description: These bits set the g range as described in Table 18: 00=±2 g, 01=±4 g, 10=±8 g, 11=±16 g.

fields.name: x_axis_data_lsb
fields.description: Least significant byte of X-axis acceleration output data in twos complement format. Controlled by DATA_FORMAT register (Address 0x31).

fields.name: x_msb
fields.description: Most significant byte of the X-axis acceleration output data in two's complement format. Part of the 16-bit X-axis value with DATAX0 (0x32) as LSB.

fields.name: datay_7_0_
fields.description: Least significant byte of Y-axis output data in two's complement format. Part of 16-bit Y-axis acceleration value (DATAY0 = LSB, DATAY1 = MSB). Data format controlled by DATA_FORMAT register (0x31).

fields.name: y_data_msb
fields.description: Most significant byte of Y-axis acceleration output data in twos complement format. Part of 16-bit Y-axis value combined with DATAY0 (LSB at 0x34). Data format controlled by DATA_FORMAT register (0x31).

fields.name: zdata
fields.description: Least significant byte of Z-axis output data in twos complement format. Format controlled by DATA_FORMAT register (0x31).

fields.name: z_15_8_
fields.description: Most significant byte of the Z-axis acceleration output data in twos complement format. Format controlled by DATA_FORMAT register (0x31).

fields.name: fifo_mode
fields.description: These bits set the FIFO mode: 00 = Bypass (FIFO is bypassed), 01 = FIFO (collects up to 32 values then stops, collects new data only when not full), 10 = Stream (holds last 32 values, overwrites oldest when full), 11 = Trigger (holds last data samples before trigger then collects until full).

fields.name: trigger
fields.description: Trigger bit for trigger mode: 0 links trigger event to INT1, 1 links to INT2.

fields.name: samples
fields.description: Samples bits function depends on FIFO mode (Table 20): Bypass=none; FIFO/Stream=number of entries for watermark interrupt; Trigger=number of samples retained before trigger. Value 0 sets watermark immediately; undesirable in trigger mode.

fields.name: fifo_trig
fields.description: A 1 in the FIFO_TRIG bit corresponds to a trigger event occurring, and a 0 means that a FIFO trigger event has not occurred.

fields.name: rsvd
fields.description: Reserved bit, always reads as 0.

fields.name: entries
fields.description: These bits report how many data values are stored in FIFO. FIFO stores a maximum of 32 entries, which equates to a maximum of 33 entries available at any given time because an additional entry is available at the output filter of the device.
